name: Chicken and Sausage Gumbo
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Chicken breasts, split in half and boned
recipeInstructions:
1. Season chicken breasts lightly with salt, black pepper, and garlic powder. If possible, do this the day before you cook the gumbo.
2. Slice sausage into 1/4-to-1/2 inch pieces.
3. Make roux: In a cast iron or very heavy skillet that is very clean, heat oil over high heat until it just begins to smoke. Gradually stir in flour using a long-handled spoon.
4. Cook the roux for about 3 to 4 minutes, stirring constantly so it does not burn. If black specks appear, discard them and start again.
5. Remove the roux from heat and stir in onions, green peppers, and celery. Stir until the mixture stops getting darker (about 1 minute).
6. Return the skillet to low heat and add enough water to cover the ingredients by about an inch. Cook for five to seven minutes, stirring constantly.
7. In a large Dutch oven, bring 6 cups of water to a boil. Gradually add the roux, stirring to dissolve it thoroughly.
8. Add chicken and cook for about 30 to 40 minutes or until cooked through. Remove chicken and set aside to cool. Debone and cut into bite-size pieces.
9. Return the cooled chicken to the pot. Add sausage, remaining water, black pepper, and garlic. Simmer uncovered for 35 to 45 minutes, stirring frequently.
10. Taste and add salt if necessary. Stir in the reserved chicken and remove the gumbo from heat. Skim surface fat with a spoon.
11. Serve over rice with a tossed salad and French bread.
